acceptance_targets: [CH03-CRAFT-001] PASS if the revised passage around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:375 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:393 shows (1) the private rule and (2) an immediate conflicting pressure/action that leaves her morally exposed on-page; FAIL if the scene ends that beat as a clean self-exonerating vow without a concrete counterforce.

[CH03-DIALOGUE-001] PASS if, in the revised cited lines, Dohyun uses labor/neighborhood concretes in at least three lines and contains no more than one abstract noun cluster per line; FAIL if his speech can still be swapped with Ina/Kwon without noticeable idiolect loss.

[CH03-DIALOGUE-002] PASS if the revised exchange includes at least one clearly distinct rhythm marker per speaker in the cited span (Hyejin task-first anecdotal line, Ina self-corrective line, Dohyun short declarative before memory spill); FAIL if the three voices still read as interchangeable polished commentary.

[CH03-PROSE-001] PASS if no more than two laugh-formula constructions remain across the chapter and each remaining one serves a distinct emotional function in context; FAIL if three or more similarly shaped laugh descriptors still recur.

[CH03-CRAFT-002] PASS if the revised cited span preserves all three operational facts (Friday 18:00 cutoff, Monday 09:00 filing, one-time pre-noon hold) while limiting any single uninterrupted legal speech block to three sentences and adding at least two concrete reaction beats in between; FAIL otherwise.

[CH03-BOOKER-001] PASS if, on reread of the revised spans, each call changes Ina's option set in a non-overlapping way (call one = immediate hold decision; call two = stipend seizure consequence) and the second call cannot be cut without losing new causal information; FAIL if the second call mainly repeats the first call's threat language.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:375 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:393 shows (1) the private rule and (2) an immediate conflicting pressure/action that leaves her morally exposed on-page; FAIL if the scene ends that beat as a clean self-exonerating vow without a concrete counterforce.
findings.problem: Sanitization-adjacent moral safety valve: immediately after coercive lock-in pressure, Ina's private ethics list functions as reader reassurance ('I still have principles') more than scene-level risk, which softens the ugliness of complicity at the exact decision point.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the commitment beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:375, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:381, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:383, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:393 so the red line remains but is immediately stress-tested by a concrete near-term compromise or cost (financial, legal, or operational) rather than landing as a standalone ethical disclaimer.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, in the revised cited lines, Dohyun uses labor/neighborhood concretes in at least three lines and contains no more than one abstract noun cluster per line; FAIL if his speech can still be swapped with Ina/Kwon without noticeable idiolect loss.
findings.problem: Dohyun's dialogue drifts into abstract policy rhetoric ('narrative,' 'policy,' 'purpose,' 'duty') that conflicts with his style-bible idiolect (manual-labor/neighborhood grounded speech, sparse declaratives, confessional memory bursts). Voice edges toward Ina/Kwon register instead of staying distinct.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework Dohyun's lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:97, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:101, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:127, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:143 with dock/shift/street-level vocabulary, shorter baseline replies, and one concrete memory chain that carries his wound without policy-language abstraction.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised exchange includes at least one clearly distinct rhythm marker per speaker in the cited span (Hyejin task-first anecdotal line, Ina self-corrective line, Dohyun short declarative before memory spill); FAIL if the three voices still read as interchangeable polished commentary.
findings.problem: In the dinner-pressure exchange, Ina/Hyejin/Dohyun often share similarly polished aphoristic cadence, reducing register separation under stress. The scene needs more interruption, asymmetry, and character-specific rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise exchange blocks anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:93 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:139 so Hyejin lands advice through task/anecdote, Ina shows qualifier-then-correction stress pattern, and Dohyun alternates sparse replies with one over-disclosure memory turn.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than two laugh-formula constructions remain across the chapter and each remaining one serves a distinct emotional function in context; FAIL if three or more similarly shaped laugh descriptors still recur.
findings.problem: Recurring reaction formula appears more than twice: laugh verb plus adjective bundle ('rough two-note,' 'short and unwilling,' 'brief, dry,' 'short and tired'). This becomes a detectable prose tic and flattens emotional texture.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:165, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:241, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:353, replace at least two laugh-formula beats with different embodied reactions (breath shift, gesture, aborted speech, silence, object interaction) while preserving scene intent.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised cited span preserves all three operational facts (Friday 18:00 cutoff, Monday 09:00 filing, one-time pre-noon hold) while limiting any single uninterrupted legal speech block to three sentences and adding at least two concrete reaction beats in between; FAIL otherwise.
findings.problem: Late-scene debt/legal mechanics are accurate but over-serialized, with long uninterrupted procedural blocks that dilute embodied consequence and create transcript cadence during a high-stakes emotional beat.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten and redistribute legal exposition in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:331, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:339, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:357 by interleaving shorter disclosures with immediate physical/decision reactions from Ina.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, on reread of the revised spans, each call changes Ina's option set in a non-overlapping way (call one = immediate hold decision; call two = stipend seizure consequence) and the second call cannot be cut without losing new causal information; FAIL if the second call mainly repeats the first call's threat language.
findings.problem: Mid-to-late chapter escalation loops similar debt-threat information twice before the irreversible decision beat, reducing turn sharpness and narrative propulsion in the second scene.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reshape the escalation arc around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:221 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:331 so call one establishes baseline jeopardy and call two introduces genuinely new leverage tied to the stipend decision, rather than re-stating deadline pressure.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when stakes demand it: show coercion mechanics, sexual negotiation, grief rituals, and financial desperation at scene level. In edge cases, prefer precision over decorum; protect character dignity through perspective accuracy, not by omitting the hard material.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene requires bodily, economic, or moral consequence and the draft retreats into abstract phrasing, rewrite with concrete action, sensation, and decision impact before proceeding.
aesthetic_risk_policy.operational_resolution_rule: When uncertain between tasteful distance and narrative truth, choose the version that preserves causal accountability and embodied consequence, then tune language for clarity rather than softness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, legal language, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter structures deliberately: high-density dialogue vs interior reflection, multi-scene public collision vs two-scene intimate pressure, aftermath inventory vs anticipatory dread. Never let two consecutive chapters share the same pacing architecture or turn mechanism.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsentimental literary diction that permits technical vocabulary, profanity, and class-marked speech without flattening idiolects.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person centered on Seo Ina, with brief controlled widenings in public-event chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternating pressure waves: compressed clauses in operational stress, longer breath-based lines in grief and reflection.
